description: Contract-backed smoke proof for the read-biased draining-region snapshot cache, including fallback evidence when write-heavy bursts make the cached path lose.

smoke_scenarios.description: Warm the cached draining-region snapshot once, then measure steady-state read latency with no further writes.

notes: Execute mode is deterministic because the proof drives RuntimeState directly without external IO.
Reader latency numbers are informative only; the pinned contract freezes qualitative cache-hit behavior and correctness.

template_env.READ_BIASED_REGION_SNAPSHOT_SMOKE_ARTIFA: Override the retrieved JSON report root.

recommended_for: Governor snapshots dominated by repeated reads of a mostly-stable region table
Low-write runtime phases where cached draining counts avoid repeated O(N) scans
avoid_when: Bursts that cross the write-heavy threshold before every snapshot
Phases where writer-side adjustment overhead dominates steady-state reads

capture_plan: Warm the cached path with one authoritative read after setup, then record steady-state read latency and correctness checksums.
Persist the report JSON and freeze the projection fields that prove the cache stays profitable under read-heavy access.

smoke_scenarios.description: Cross the write-heavy threshold before every snapshot and prove the fallback scan remains pinned while correctness matches the baseline.

notes: Execute mode is deterministic because each sample constructs a fresh RuntimeState and performs one snapshot read.
This scenario is the negative control: it proves exactly when the cached path should lose and why the fallback stays pinned.

template_env.READ_BIASED_REGION_SNAPSHOT_SMOKE_ARTIFA: Override the retrieved JSON report root.

recommended_for: Validating that the conservative scan still wins under a burst of counted-region transitions
Auditing writer-stall and fallback-scan evidence before enabling the cache by default
avoid_when: You only want the positive cache-hit path and do not need the pinned fallback evidence
A different write-heavy threshold is being evaluated and the contract has not been updated

capture_plan: Create a fresh write-heavy burst for each sample, then record reader latency, writer adjustment time, and correctness checksums.
Persist the report JSON and freeze the projection fields that prove the fallback remains pinned once the threshold is crossed.
